summary: Kevin O'Brien from Aegis spoke with Vanessa Cruz at Coastal Living about deploying Aegis Protect for their hybrid backup and recovery environment following a successful spring collection launch. Vanessa described a fragmented backup setup across CloudPrime and on-prem systems totaling 80-100TB across 40-50 workloads, with poor visibility being the primary pain point. Kevin outlined a phased three-part implementation plan, confirmed compatibility with their infrastructure, and discussed RPO/RTO requirements for their critical customer data platform. The call concluded with plans for a kickoff meeting the following Wednesday and next steps around onboarding documentation.
actionItems: Kevin O'Brien: Send formal onboarding plan document including three-phase timeline, resource requirements, and business impact analysis template by end of day tomorrow
Kevin O'Brien: Send calendar invite for Wednesday kickoff call with implementation lead copied in
Vanessa Cruz: Loop in two senior engineers for the Wednesday kickoff call
Vanessa Cruz: Schedule a follow-up conversation about Aegis Comply v2 and PCI DSS reporting after Protect is up and running

keyMoments.text: Vanessa identifies fragmented dashboards and lack of unified visibility as her biggest pain point during peak seasons
keyMoments.speaker: Vanessa Cruz

keyMoments.text: Vanessa confirms RPO of one hour and RTO of four hours for the customer data platform, expressing concern about downtime during peak sale events like Black Friday
keyMoments.speaker: Vanessa Cruz

keyMoments.text: Vanessa reacts positively to the phased onboarding approach and lighter-than-expected engineering time commitment
keyMoments.speaker: Vanessa Cruz

keyMoments.text: Vanessa praises the conversation quality, noting it was one of the more energizing vendor conversations she has had
keyMoments.speaker: Vanessa Cruz
